### AVPV/PeN kisspeptin neuron, mouse `http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020073`

- [AVPV/PeN kisspeptin neuron, mouse](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020073) [present in taxon](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002175) [Mus musculus](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/NCBITaxon_10090)

- [AVPV/PeN kisspeptin neuron, mouse](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020073) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "A hypothalamic kisspeptin neuron with its soma located in the anteroventral periventricular nucleus (AVPV) and the posterior part of the periventricular hypothalamic nucleus in the rostral hypothalamus (Stephens et al., 2017; Nejad et al., 2017). It mediates estrogen positive feedback by driving gonadotropin-releasing hormone (GnRH) and luteinizing hormone (LH) secretion and is required for the preovulatory LH surge (Adachi et al., 2007; Nejad et al., 2017). In mice, it co-expresses kisspeptin (Kiss1) and tyrosine hydroxylase (TH), indicating a dopaminergic component within an otherwise primarily GABAergic phenotype (Kauffman et al., 2007; Stephens et al., 2017, 2024). It also expresses estrogen receptor alpha (Esr1), enabling direct regulation by estradiol (Frazao et al., 2013; Wartenberg et al., 2021). It belongs to a sexually dimorphic population, with higher cell numbers observed in females than in males in rodents (Stephens et al., 2017; Nejad et al., 2017)." 
  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "PMID:17213691" 

  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "PMID:34561233" 

  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "PMID:29201072"

- [Brodmann area](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0013529) [id](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#id) "UBERON:0013529" 

- [Brodmann area](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0013529) [prefLabel](http://www.w3.org/2004/02/skos/core#prefLabel) "Brodmann area" 

- [Brodmann area](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0013529) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "A segmentation of the cerebral cortex on the basis of cytoarchitecture as described in Brodmann-1905, Brodmann-1909 and Brodmann-10. Maps for several species were presented. NeuroNames includes only areas in the human and in Old World monkeys. Of the latter, Brodmann studied representatives of several species including guenons (one Cercopithecus mona, one Cercocebus torquatus, and one Cercopithecus otherwise unspecified), which are all closely related African species, and one macaque (Macaca mulatta) an Asian species (Brodmann-1905). The legend to the summary map in Brodmann-1909 ascribes the areas simply to Cercopithecus. Brodmann referenced the areas by name and number. The same area number in humans and monkeys did not necessarily refer to topologically or cytoarchitecturally homologous structures. In NeuroNames the standard term for human areas consists of the English translation of Brodmann's Latin name followed by the number he assigned, e.g., agranular frontal area 6; the standard terms for monkey areas are in the format: area 6 of Brodmann-1909. He mapped a portion of areas limited to the banks of sulci, e.g., area 3 of Brodmann-1909 (Brodmann-1909) onto the adjacent, visible surface. This accounts for the fact that some areas appear larger on his surface map than on maps of other authors, e.g., area 3 of Vogts-1919. (Adapted from NeuroNames)." 
  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "BIRNLEX:1731" 

- [Brodmann area](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0013529) [has_related_synonym](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasRelatedSynonym) "Brodmann parcellation scheme region" 
  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "BIRNLEX:1731" 

- [Brodmann area](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0013529) [has_related_synonym](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasRelatedSynonym) "Brodmann partition scheme region" 
  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "BIRNLEX:1731" 

- [Brodmann area](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0013529) [has_related_synonym](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasRelatedSynonym) "Brodmann's areas" 
  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "BIRNLEX:1731" 

  - [has_synonym_type](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasSynonymType) [plural form](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/OMO_0003004) 

- [Brodmann area](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0013529) [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "neuronames:1032" 
  - [source](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#source) "tgbugs" 

- [Brodmann area](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0013529) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "Brodmann area" 

- [Brodmann area](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0013529) SubClassOf [regional part of brain](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0002616) 

- [Brodmann area](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0013529) SubClassOf [part of](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000050) some [cerebral cortex](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0000956) 
  - [source](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#source) "FMA"

### CA1 pyramidal cell `http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020072`

#### Added
- Class: [CA1 pyramidal cell](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020072) 

- [CA1 pyramidal cell](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020072) [term tracker item](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000233) "https://github.com/obophenotype/cell-ontology/issues/3646" 

- [CA1 pyramidal cell](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020072) [creator](http://purl.org/dc/elements/1.1/creator) "GitHub Copilot" 

- [CA1 pyramidal cell](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020072) [contributor](http://purl.org/dc/terms/contributor) [0000-0002-5507-2103](https://orcid.org/0000-0002-5507-2103) 

- [CA1 pyramidal cell](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020072) [date](http://purl.org/dc/terms/date) "2026-05-21T09:55:20Z"^^[dateTime](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#dateTime) 

- [CA1 pyramidal cell](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020072) [in_subset](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#inSubset) [human_subset](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/cl#human_subset) 

- [CA1 pyramidal cell](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020072) [in_subset](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#inSubset) [mouse_subset](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/cl#mouse_subset) 

- [CA1 pyramidal cell](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020072) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "A hippocampal pyramidal neuron with soma located in the pyramidal layer of hippocampal area CA1 (Cembrowski et al., 2016; M&uuml;ller &amp; Remy, 2017), forming the dominant excitatory projection population of the CA1 subfield. As the primary output cell of Ammon's horn, it projects via the subiculum to entorhinal cortex and is capable of glutamate secretion as a neurotransmitter (Dale et al., 2015). In mouse, it is distinguished from CA2 and CA3 pyramidal neurons by soma position within the CA1 stratum pyramidale and by expression of wolframin (Wfs1), an endoplasmic reticulum-resident membrane protein enriched in deep-sublayer CA1 cells (Cembrowski et al., 2016)." 
  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "PMID:29250747" 

  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "PMID:27113915" 

  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "PMID:26777276" 

  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "PMID:26346726" 

- [CA1 pyramidal cell](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020072) [has exact synonym](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasExactSynonym) "CA1 pyramidal neuron" 
  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "PMID:27113915" 

- [CA1 pyramidal cell](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020072) [has exact synonym](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasExactSynonym) "pyramidal cell of CA1" 
  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "PMID:27113915" 

- [CA1 pyramidal cell](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020072) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "CA1 pyramidal cell" 

- [CA1 pyramidal cell](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020072) SubClassOf [glutamatergic neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0000679) 

- [CA1 pyramidal cell](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020072) SubClassOf [hippocampal pyramidal neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_1001571) 

- [CA1 pyramidal cell](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020072) SubClassOf [has soma location](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002100) some [pyramidal layer of CA1](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0014548)

### CA2 pyramidal cell `http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020070`

#### Added
- Class: [CA2 pyramidal cell](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020070) 

- [CA2 pyramidal cell](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020070) [term tracker item](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000233) [3651](https://github.com/obophenotype/cell-ontology/issues/3651) 

- [CA2 pyramidal cell](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020070) [contributor](http://purl.org/dc/terms/contributor) [0000-0002-5507-2103](https://orcid.org/0000-0002-5507-2103) 

- [CA2 pyramidal cell](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020070) [creator](http://purl.org/dc/terms/creator) "GitHub Copilot" 

- [CA2 pyramidal cell](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020070) [date](http://purl.org/dc/terms/date) "2026-05-26T13:30:22Z"^^[dateTime](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#dateTime) 

- [CA2 pyramidal cell](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020070) [in_subset](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#inSubset) [human_subset](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/cl#human_subset) 

- [CA2 pyramidal cell](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020070) [in_subset](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#inSubset) [mouse_subset](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/cl#mouse_subset) 

- [CA2 pyramidal cell](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020070) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "A hippocampal pyramidal neuron with soma located in the pyramidal layer of hippocampal area CA2 (Cembrowski et al., 2016; Sanchez-Aguilera et al., 2021). Distinguished from CA1 and CA3 pyramidal neurons by expression of Pcp4, Rgs14, and Amigo2 in rodents (San Antonio et al., 2014; Caruana et al., 2012), and by absence of the CA1 marker wolframin (Wfs1) (Evans et al., 2018). Receives strong LTP-competent input from entorhinal cortex at distal dendrites, while Schaffer collateral inputs to proximal dendrites are resistant to canonical LTP in wild-type animals (Chevaleyre &amp; Siegelbaum, 2010). Required for social memory in mice (Hitti &amp; Siegelbaum, 2014)." 
  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "PMID:22904370" 

  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "PMID:27113915" 

  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "PMID:24166578" 

  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "PMID:20510860" 

  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "PMID:29911178" 

  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "PMID:33956790" 

  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "PMID:24572357" 

- [CA2 pyramidal cell](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020070) [has exact synonym](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasExactSynonym) "CA2 pyramidal neuron" 
  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "PMID:27113915" 

- [CA2 pyramidal cell](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020070) [has exact synonym](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasExactSynonym) "pyramidal cell of CA2" 
  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "PMID:27113915" 

- [CA2 pyramidal cell](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020070) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "CA2 pyramidal cell" 

- [CA2 pyramidal cell](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020070) SubClassOf [glutamatergic neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0000679) 

- [CA2 pyramidal cell](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020070) SubClassOf [hippocampal pyramidal neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_1001571) 

- [CA2 pyramidal cell](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020070) SubClassOf [has soma location](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002100) some [pyramidal layer of CA2](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0014549)

### CA3 pyramidal cell `http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020068`

#### Added
- Class: [CA3 pyramidal cell](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020068) 

- [CA3 pyramidal cell](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020068) [contributor](http://purl.org/dc/terms/contributor) [0000-0002-5507-2103](https://orcid.org/0000-0002-5507-2103) 

- [CA3 pyramidal cell](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020068) [date](http://purl.org/dc/terms/date) "2026-05-26T14:55:38Z"^^[dateTime](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#dateTime) 

- [CA3 pyramidal cell](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020068) [in_subset](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#inSubset) [human_subset](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/cl#human_subset) 

- [CA3 pyramidal cell](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020068) [in_subset](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#inSubset) [mouse_subset](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/cl#mouse_subset) 

- [CA3 pyramidal cell](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020068) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "A hippocampal pyramidal neuron with soma located in the pyramidal layer of hippocampal area CA3 (Cembrowski et al., 2016; Wheeler et al., 2015). Capable of glutamate secretion as a neurotransmitter (Dale et al., 2015) and distinguished from CA1 and CA2 pyramidal neurons by thorny excrescences on the proximal apical dendrites &mdash; postsynaptic specializations that receive excitatory mossy fiber input from dentate gyrus granule cells (Munster-Wandowski et al., 2013). Harbours a dense recurrent Schaffer collateral network; individual neurons can act as hub neurons capable of triggering hippocampal network bursts (Marissal et al., 2012)." 
  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "PMID:27113915" 

  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "PMID:23271650" 

  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "PMID:26346726" 

  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "PMID:26402459" 

  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "PMID:24319410" 

- [CA3 pyramidal cell](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020068) [has exact synonym](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasExactSynonym) "CA3 pyramidal neuron" 
  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "PMID:27113915" 

- [CA3 pyramidal cell](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020068) [has exact synonym](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasExactSynonym) "pyramidal cell of CA3" 
  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "PMID:27113915" 

- [CA3 pyramidal cell](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020068) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "CA3 pyramidal cell" 

- [CA3 pyramidal cell](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020068) SubClassOf [glutamatergic neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0000679) 

- [CA3 pyramidal cell](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020068) SubClassOf [hippocampal pyramidal neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_1001571) 

- [CA3 pyramidal cell](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020068) SubClassOf [has soma location](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002100) some [pyramidal layer of CA3](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0014550)

### DNA catabolic process `http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/GO_0006308`
#### Removed
- [DNA catabolic process](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/GO_0006308) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "The cellular DNA metabolic process resulting in the breakdown of DNA, deoxyribonucleic acid, one of the two main types of nucleic acid, consisting of a long unbranched macromolecule formed from one or two strands of linked deoxyribonucleotides, the 3'-phosphate group of each constituent deoxyribonucleotide being joined in 3',5'-phosphodiester linkage to the 5'-hydroxyl group of the deoxyribose moiety of the next one." 
  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "GOC:go_curators" 

  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "ISBN:0198506732"

- [Oriens-Lacunosum Moleculare neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020074) [creator](http://purl.org/dc/elements/1.1/creator) "GitHub Copilot" 

- [Oriens-Lacunosum Moleculare neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020074) [date](http://purl.org/dc/terms/date) "2026-04-10T10:48:46Z"^^[dateTime](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#dateTime) 

- [Oriens-Lacunosum Moleculare neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020074) [in_subset](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#inSubset) [human_subset](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/cl#human_subset) 

- [Oriens-Lacunosum Moleculare neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020074) [in_subset](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#inSubset) [mouse_subset](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/cl#mouse_subset) 

- [Oriens-Lacunosum Moleculare neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020074) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "A GABAergic interneuron of the hippocampal CA1 region whose horizontally oriented soma is located in the stratum oriens at the stratum oriens&ndash;alveus border, with spiny dendrites confined to stratum oriens; the axon ascends vertically to arborize exclusively in stratum lacunosum-moleculare, innervating the apical tufts of pyramidal neurons. Electrophysiologically, these cells display regular-to-adapting spiking with a prominent hyperpolarization-activated cation current (Ih) and theta-frequency resonance. In rodents, canonical markers include somatostatin (Sst) and metabotropic glutamate receptor 1 (Grm1/mGluR1); the alpha-2 nicotinic acetylcholine receptor subunit (Chrna2) is a highly specific marker in mouse." 
  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "PMID:29487503" 

  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "PMID:31420995" 

  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "PMID:23042082" 

  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "PMID:10804195" 

  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "PMID:20421280" 

  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "PMID:33361464" 

- [Oriens-Lacunosum Moleculare neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020074) [has exact synonym](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasExactSynonym) "OLM neuron" 
  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "PMID:20421280"

### animal organ morphogenesis `http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/GO_0009887`
#### Removed
- [animal organ morphogenesis](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/GO_0009887) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "Morphogenesis of an animal organ. An organ is defined as a tissue or set of tissues that work together to perform a specific function or functions. Morphogenesis is the process in which anatomical structures are generated and organized. Organs are commonly observed as visibly distinct structures, but may also exist as loosely associated clusters of cells that work together to perform a specific function or functions."

### apical junction complex `http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/GO_0043296`
#### Removed
- [apical junction complex](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/GO_0043296) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "A functional unit located near the cell apex at the points of contact between epithelial cells, which in vertebrates is composed of the tight junction, the zonula adherens, and desmosomes and in some invertebrates, such as Drosophila, is composed of the subapical complex (SAC), the zonula adherens and the septate junction. Functions in the regulation of cell polarity, tissue integrity and intercellular adhesion and permeability."

### apoptotic process `http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/GO_0006915`
#### Removed
- [apoptotic process](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/GO_0006915) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "A programmed cell death process which begins when a cell receives an internal (e.g. DNA damage) or external signal (e.g. an extracellular death ligand), and proceeds through a series of biochemical events (signaling pathway phase) which trigger an execution phase. The execution phase is the last step of an apoptotic process, and is typically characterized by rounding-up of the cell, retraction of pseudopodes, reduction of cellular volume (pyknosis), chromatin condensation, nuclear fragmentation (karyorrhexis), plasma membrane blebbing and fragmentation of the cell into apoptotic bodies. When the execution phase is completed, the cell has died." 
  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "PMID:18846107" 

  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "GOC:go_curators" 

  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "GOC:tb" 

  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "GOC:cjm" 

  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "GOC:ecd" 

  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "PMID:21494263" 

  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "GOC:dhl" 

  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "GOC:mtg_apoptosis" 

  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "ISBN:0198506732" 

#### Added
- [apoptotic process](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/GO_0006915) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "A programmed cell death process which begins when a cell receives an internal (e.g. DNA damage) or external signal (e.g. an extracellular death ligand), and proceeds through a series of biochemical events (signaling pathway phase) which trigger an execution phase. The execution phase is the last step of an apoptotic process, and is typically characterized by rounding-up of the cell, retraction of pseudopodes, reduction of cellular volume (pyknosis), chromatin condensation, nuclear fragmentation (karyorrhexis), plasma membrane blebbing and fragmentation of the cell into apoptotic bodies. When the execution phase is completed, the cell has died."

### cell cycle `http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/GO_0007049`
#### Removed
- [cell cycle](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/GO_0007049) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "The progression of biochemical and morphological phases and events that occur in a cell during successive cell replication or nuclear replication events. Canonically, the cell cycle comprises the replication and segregation of genetic material followed by the division of the cell, but in endocycles or syncytial cells nuclear replication or nuclear division may not be followed by cell division."

- [ciliary basal body-plasma membrane docking](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/GO_0097711) [has exact synonym](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasExactSynonym) "ciliary basal body docking" 

- [ciliary basal body-plasma membrane docking](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/GO_0097711) [has_obo_namespace](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasOBONamespace) "biological_process" 

- [ciliary basal body-plasma membrane docking](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/GO_0097711) [has_related_synonym](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasRelatedSynonym) "anchoring of the basal body to the plasma membrane" 

- [ciliary basal body-plasma membrane docking](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/GO_0097711) [id](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#id) "GO:0097711" 

- [ciliary basal body-plasma membrane docking](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/GO_0097711) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "The docking of a cytosolic centriole/basal body to the plasma membrane via the ciliary transition fibers. In some species this may happen via an intermediate step, by first docking to the ciliary vesicle via the ciliary transition fibers. The basal body-ciliary vesicle then relocates to the plasma membrane, followed by the ciliary vesicle fusing with the plasma membrane, effectively attaching the basal body to the plasma membrane." 
  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "PMID:25686250" 

  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "GOC:cilia" 

  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "PMID:26981235" 

  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "PMID:23348840" 

  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "PMID:23530209" 

  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "PMID:13978319" 

- [ciliary basal body-plasma membrane docking](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/GO_0097711) [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "Reactome:R-HSA-5620912" 
  - [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "Anchoring of the basal body to the plasma membrane" 

- [ciliary basal body-plasma membrane docking](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/GO_0097711) [comment](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#comment) "Basal bodies in jawed vertebrates appear to first attach to a ciliary vesicle. It is unclear how specific this is to jawed vertebrates or if other organisms also employ this sequence. Some species like Giardia intestinalis do not relocate their basal bodies to the plasma membrane, but have their axonemes extend through the cytosol to then protrude out of the cell to form flagella." 

- [ciliary basal body-plasma membrane docking](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/GO_0097711) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "ciliary basal body-plasma membrane docking" 

- [ciliary basal body-plasma membrane docking](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/GO_0097711) SubClassOf [organelle localization by membrane tethering](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/GO_0140056) 

- [ciliary basal body-plasma membrane docking](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/GO_0097711) SubClassOf not ([in taxon](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002162) some [Ascomycota](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/NCBITaxon_4890)) 

- [ciliary basal body-plasma membrane docking](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/GO_0097711) SubClassOf [part of](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/BFO_0000050) some [cilium assembly](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/GO_0060271)

- [detection of visible light](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/GO_0009584) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "The series of events in which a visible light stimulus is received by a cell and converted into a molecular signal. A visible light stimulus is electromagnetic radiation that can be perceived visually by an organism; for organisms lacking a visual system, this can be defined as light with a wavelength within the range 380 to 780 nm." 
  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "GOC:go_curators" 

  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "ISBN:0198506732"

A visible light stimulus is electromagnetic radiation that can be perceived visually by an organism; for organisms lacking a visual system, this can be defined as light with a wavelength within the range 380 to 780 nm."

### embryo development `http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/GO_0009790`
#### Removed
- [embryo development](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/GO_0009790) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "The process whose specific outcome is the progression of an embryo from its formation until the end of its embryonic life stage. The end of the embryonic stage is organism-specific. For example, for mammals, the process would begin with zygote formation and end with birth. For insects, the process would begin at zygote formation and end with larval hatching. For plant zygotic embryos, this would be from zygote formation to the end of seed dormancy. For plant vegetative embryos, this would be from the initial determination of the cell or group of cells to form an embryo until the point when the embryo becomes independent of the parent plant." 
  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "GOC:isa_complete" 

  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "GOC:go_curators" 

  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "GOC:mtg_sensu" 

#### Added
- [embryo development](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/GO_0009790) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "The process whose specific outcome is the progression of an embryo from its formation until the end of its embryonic life stage. The end of the embryonic stage is organism-specific. For example, for mammals, the process would begin with zygote formation and end with birth. For insects, the process would begin at zygote formation and end with larval hatching. For plant zygotic embryos, this would be from zygote formation to the end of seed dormancy. For plant vegetative embryos, this would be from the initial determination of the cell or group of cells to form an embryo until the point when the embryo becomes independent of the parent plant." 
  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "GOC:curators" 

  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "GOC:isa_complete" 

  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "GOC:mtg_sensu" 


### embryo development ending in birth or egg hatching `http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/GO_0009792`
#### Removed
- [embryo development ending in birth or egg hatching](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/GO_0009792) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "The process whose specific outcome is the progression of an embryo over time, from zygote formation until the end of the embryonic life stage. The end of the embryonic life stage is organism-specific and may be somewhat arbitrary; for mammals it is usually considered to be birth, for insects the hatching of the first instar larva from the eggshell."

  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "GOC:mtg_sensu"

- [entorhinal cortex](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0002728) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "Component of the temporal lobe on the mesial surface. The rostral and caudal boundaries of the entorhinal cortex are the rostral end of the collateral sulcus and the caudal end of the amygdala respectively. The medial boundary is the medial aspect of the temporal lobe and the lateral boundary is the collateral sulcus. (DK)." 
  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "Wikipedia:Entorhinal_cortex" 

  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "BIRNLEX:1508"

  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "BIRNLEX:1508" 

  - [has_synonym_type](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasSynonymType) [INCONSISTENT](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/uberon/core#INCONSISTENT)

- [entorhinal cortex layer 1](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0022336) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "Layer of the entorhinal cortex that is most superficial, continuous with layer 1 of adjacent neocortex and the molecular layer of the subiculum, lying deep to the pial layer and superficial to layer 2. It is relatively acellular consisting mostly of fibers running transversely relative to the cortical surface (Adapted from Paxinos, G. ### entorhinal cortex layer II stellate cell `http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020067`

#### Added
- Class: [entorhinal cortex layer II stellate cell](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020067) 

- [entorhinal cortex layer II stellate cell](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020067) [term tracker item](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000233) [3630](https://github.com/obophenotype/cell-ontology/issues/3630) 

- [entorhinal cortex layer II stellate cell](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020067) [date](http://purl.org/dc/terms/date) "2026-05-05T13:19:02Z"^^[dateTime](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#dateTime) 

- [entorhinal cortex layer II stellate cell](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020067) [in_subset](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#inSubset) [human_subset](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/cl#human_subset) 

- [entorhinal cortex layer II stellate cell](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020067) [in_subset](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#inSubset) [mouse_subset](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/cl#mouse_subset) 

- [entorhinal cortex layer II stellate cell](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020067) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "A glutamatergic principal neuron with its soma located in layer II of the entorhinal cortex, distinguished by stellate morphology, lack of calbindin expression, and expression of reelin in rodents. This cell type is a major source of perforant pathway projections from entorhinal cortex layer II to the dentate gyrus of the hippocampal formation. It displays electrophysiological properties including a prominent hyperpolarisation-activated inward current, voltage sag, and theta-frequency membrane resonance." 
  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "PMID:32039761" 

  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "PMID:26223342" 

  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "PMID:20512133" 

  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "PMID:31680885" 

  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "PMID:26711115"

  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "PMID:26223342"

### epidermis development `http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/GO_0008544`
#### Removed
- [epidermis development](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/GO_0008544) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "The process whose specific outcome is the progression of the epidermis over time, from its formation to the mature structure. ### extracellular region `http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/GO_0005576`
#### Removed
- [extracellular region](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/GO_0005576) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "The space external to the outermost structure of a cell. For cells without external protective or external encapsulating structures this refers to space outside of the plasma membrane. This term covers the host cell environment outside an intracellular parasite." 
  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "GOC:go_curators" 

- [extracellular region](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/GO_0005576) [comment](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#comment) "Note that this term is intended to annotate gene products that are not attached to the cell surface. For gene products from multicellular organisms which are secreted from a cell but retained within the organism (i.e. released into the interstitial fluid or blood), consider the cellular component term 'extracellular space ; GO:0005615'." 

#### Added
- [extracellular region](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/GO_0005576) [term tracker item](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000233) "https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/issues/31545"^^[anyURI](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#anyURI)

- [extracellular region](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/GO_0005576) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "The space external to the outermost structure of a cell. For cells without external protective or external encapsulating structures this refers to space outside of the plasma membrane. This term covers the host cell environment outside an intracellular parasite." 
  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "GOC:curators" 

- [extracellular region](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/GO_0005576) [comment](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#comment) "Note that this term is intended to annotate gene products that are not attached to the cell surface. For gene products at the cell surface, consider annotating to external side of plasma membrane ; GO:0009897."

### fatty acid catabolic process `http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/GO_0009062`
#### Removed
- [fatty acid catabolic process](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/GO_0009062) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "The chemical reactions and pathways resulting in the breakdown of a fatty acid, any of the aliphatic monocarboxylic acids that can be liberated by hydrolysis from naturally occurring fats and oils. Fatty acids are predominantly straight-chain acids of 4 to 24 carbon atoms, which may be saturated or unsaturated; branched fatty acids and hydroxy fatty acids also occur, and very long chain acids of over 30 carbons are found in waxes."

- [gross anatomical part](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0000001) [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "CARO:0001008" 

- [gross anatomical part](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0000001) [id](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#id) "UBERON:0000001" 

- [gross anatomical part](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0000001) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "Anatomical structure that is part of a multicellular organism and is at the gross anatomical level, e.g. above the level of a cell.  Included are portions of organism substances such as blood, multi-cell-part structures such as axon tracts, acellular anatomical structures such as hair, and organism subdivisions such as head. Excluded is the whole organism and more granular parts of the organism, such as atoms, molecules, macromolecular complexes and cells." 
  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "CARO:0001008" 

- [gross anatomical part](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0000001) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "gross anatomical part" 

- [gross anatomical part](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0000001) DisjointWith [multicellular organism](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0000468) 

- [gross anatomical part](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0000001) SubClassOf [material anatomical entity](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0000465)

- [hilar mossy cell](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020069) [has exact synonym](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasExactSynonym) "mossy cell" 
  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "PMID:23420672" 

- [hilar mossy cell](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020069) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "A dentate gyrus neuron with soma in the polymorphic layer (Scharfman &amp; Myers, 2012), capable of glutamate secretion as a neurotransmitter (Scharfman &amp; Bernstein, 2015). Distinguished by a large multipolar soma, thorny excrescences on proximal dendrites, and commissural/associational axon projections terminating in the inner molecular layer of the dentate gyrus (Scharfman &amp; Myers, 2012). In rodents, serves as a major excitatory neuron of the dentate hilus providing feedback excitation to granule cells (Sun et al., 2017)." 
  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "PMID:34214666" 

  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "PMID:26347618" 

  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "PMID:33600026" 

  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "PMID:23420672" 

  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "PMID:28451637" 

- [hilar mossy cell](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020069) [has exact synonym](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasExactSynonym) "dentate hilar mossy cell" 
  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "PMID:34214666" 

- [hilar mossy cell](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020069) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "hilar mossy cell" 

- [hilar mossy cell](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020069) SubClassOf [glutamatergic neuron](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0000679)

- [ketone biosynthetic process](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/GO_0042181) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "The chemical reactions and pathways resulting in the formation of ketones, a class of organic compounds that contain the carbonyl group, CO, and in which the carbonyl group is bonded only to carbon atoms. The general formula for a ketone is RCOR, where R and R are alkyl or aryl groups." 
  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "GOC:go_curators" 

#### Added
- [ketone biosynthetic process](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/GO_0042181) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "The chemical reactions and pathways resulting in the formation of ketones, a class of organic compounds that contain the carbonyl group, CO, and in which the carbonyl group is bonded only to carbon atoms. The general formula for a ketone is RCOR, where R and R are alkyl or aryl groups." 
  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "GOC:curators"

- [lymphocyte differentiation](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/GO_0030098) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "The process in which a relatively unspecialized precursor cell acquires specialized features of a lymphocyte. ### mitochondrial gene expression `http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/GO_0140053`
#### Removed
- [mitochondrial gene expression](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/GO_0140053) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "The process in which a mitochondrial gene's sequence is converted into a mature gene product or products (proteins or RNA). This includes the production of an RNA transcript as well as any processing to produce a mature RNA product or an mRNA or circRNA (for protein-coding genes) and the translation of that mRNA or circRNA into protein. Protein maturation is included when required to form an active form of a product from an inactive precursor form." 
  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "PMID:27058308" 

#### Added
- [mitochondrial gene expression](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/GO_0140053) [term tracker item](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000233) "https://github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/issues/31659"^^[anyURI](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#anyURI) 

- [mitochondrial gene expression](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/GO_0140053) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "The process of transcribing and translating the mitochondrial genome (mtDNA) to produce at least a subset of oxidative phosphorylation (OXPHOS) proteins, 2 rRNAs, and mitochondrial tRNAs, which are critical for ATP production. Protein maturation is included when required to form an active form of a product from an inactive precursor form." 
  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "PMID:27058308" 

  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "PMID:38117001" 

  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "PMID:31861673"

- [mitochondrial translation](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/GO_0032543) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "The chemical reactions and pathways resulting in the formation of a protein in a mitochondrion. #### Removed
- [myoblast](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0000056) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "A cell that is commited to differentiating into a muscle cell. ### myoblast differentiation `http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/GO_0045445`
#### Removed
- [myoblast differentiation](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/GO_0045445) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "The process in which a relatively unspecialized cell acquires specialized features of a myoblast. A myoblast is a mononucleate cell type that, by fusion with other myoblasts, gives rise to the myotubes that eventually develop into striated muscle fibers."

#### Added
- [myoblast differentiation](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/GO_0045445) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "The process in which a relatively unspecialized cell acquires specialized features of a myoblast. A myoblast is a mononucleate cell type that, by fusion with other myoblasts, gives rise to the myotubes that eventually develop into striated muscle fibers."

- [norepinephrine transport](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/GO_0015874) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "The directed movement of norepinephrine into, out of or within a cell, or between cells, by means of some agent such as a transporter or pore. Norepinephrine (3,4-dihydroxyphenyl-2-aminoethanol) is a hormone secreted by the adrenal medulla and a neurotransmitter in the sympathetic peripheral nervous system and in some tracts of the CNS. It is also the biosynthetic precursor of epinephrine." 
  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "GOC:ai" 

  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "ISBN:0198506732"

- [norepinephrine transport](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/GO_0015874) EquivalentTo [transport](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/GO_0006810) and ([has primary input](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0004009) some [CHEBI_72587](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CHEBI_72587)) 

- [norepinephrine transport](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/GO_0015874) SubClassOf [organic cation transport](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/GO_0015695) 

- [norepinephrine transport](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/GO_0015874) SubClassOf [catecholamine transport](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/GO_0051937)

### nucleus `http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/GO_0005634`
#### Removed
- [nucleus](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/GO_0005634) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "A membrane-bounded organelle of eukaryotic cells in which chromosomes are housed and replicated. In most cells, the nucleus contains all of the cell's chromosomes except the organellar chromosomes, and is the site of RNA synthesis and processing. In some species, or in specialized cell types, RNA metabolism or DNA replication may be absent." 
  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "GOC:go_curators"

In some species, or in specialized cell types, RNA metabolism or DNA replication may be absent."

- [parahippocampal gyrus](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0002973) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "Component of the temporal lobe on the mesial surface, posterior to the entorhinal cortex. The rostral and caudal boundaries are the posterior end of the netorhinal cortex and the caudal portion of the hippocampus, respectively. The medial boudnary is designated as the medial aspect off the temporal lobe and the lateral boundary is the collateral sulcus (Christine Fennema-Notestine)."

### parvalbumin-positive basket cell `http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020071`

#### Added
- Class: [parvalbumin-positive basket cell](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020071) 

- [parvalbumin-positive basket cell](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020071) [term tracker item](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000233) [3644](https://github.com/obophenotype/cell-ontology/issues/3644) 

- [parvalbumin-positive basket cell](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020071) [contributor](http://purl.org/dc/terms/contributor) [0000-0002-5507-2103](https://orcid.org/0000-0002-5507-2103) 

- [parvalbumin-positive basket cell](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020071) [date](http://purl.org/dc/terms/date) "2026-05-19T14:15:42Z"^^[dateTime](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#dateTime) 

- [parvalbumin-positive basket cell](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020071) [in_subset](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#inSubset) [human_subset](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/cl#human_subset) 

- [parvalbumin-positive basket cell](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020071) [in_subset](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#inSubset) [mouse_subset](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/cl#mouse_subset) 

- [parvalbumin-positive basket cell](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020071) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "A basket cell of the hippocampal formation that expresses the calcium-binding protein parvalbumin (Pvalb) in rodents (Rivera et al., 2014; Que et al., 2021; Contreras et al., 2019). Somata are located in the pyramidal layers of CA1 and CA3 and the granule cell layer of the dentate gyrus. Axon collaterals are confined to the pyramidal layer, forming dense perisomatic inhibitory synapses on the somata of principal neurons; a single cell contacts more than 1500 pyramidal neurons (Sik et al., 1995). This cell is a fast-spiking GABAergic interneuron and is distinguished from CCK basket cells by absence of cannabinoid receptor 1 (Cnr1) expression (Whissell et al., 2015)." 
  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "PMID:33398060" 

  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "PMID:26441554" 

  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "PMID:7472426" 

  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "PMID:31297048" 

  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "PMID:25018703" 

- [parvalbumin-positive basket cell](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020071) [has exact synonym](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasExactSynonym) "PV basket cell" 
  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "PMID:31297048" 

- [parvalbumin-positive basket cell](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020071) [has exact synonym](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasExactSynonym) "Parvalbumin (PV) positive basket cell" 
  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "PMID:31297048" 

- [parvalbumin-positive basket cell](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020071) [label](http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#label) "parvalbumin-positive basket cell" 

- [parvalbumin-positive basket cell](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0020071) SubClassOf [basket cell](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/CL_0000118)

### plastid membrane `http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/GO_0042170`
#### Removed
- [plastid membrane](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/GO_0042170) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "Either of the lipid bilayers that surround a plastid and form the plastid envelope." 
  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "GOC:mah" 

#### Added
- [plastid membrane](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/GO_0042170) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "Any lipid bilayer that surrounds a plastid or is part of the plastid envelope. Primary plastids (in plants, green algae, red algae, and glaucophytes) are surrounded by two membranes; complex plastids derived from secondary endosymbiosis are surrounded by three or four membranes." 
  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "GOC:mah" 

  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "PMID:20124341"

### plastid translation `http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/GO_0032544`
#### Removed
- [plastid translation](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/GO_0032544) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "The chemical reactions and pathways resulting in the formation of a protein in a plastid. This is a ribosome-mediated process in which the information in messenger RNA (mRNA) is used to specify the sequence of amino acids in the protein; the plastid has its own ribosomes and transfer RNAs, and uses a genetic code that differs from the nuclear code." 
  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "GOC:go_curators" 

#### Added
- [plastid translation](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/GO_0032544) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "The chemical reactions and pathways resulting in the formation of a protein in a plastid. This is a ribosome-mediated process in which the information in messenger RNA (mRNA) is used to specify the sequence of amino acids in the protein; the plastid has its own ribosomes and transfer RNAs, and uses a genetic code that differs from the nuclear code." 
  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "GOC:curators"

### protein import into mitochondrial matrix `http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/GO_0030150`
#### Removed
- [protein import into mitochondrial matrix](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/GO_0030150) [term tracker item](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000233) "/github.com/geneontology/go-ontology/issues/29962"^^[anyURI](http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#anyURI) 

- [protein import into mitochondrial matrix](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/GO_0030150) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "The import of proteins across the outer and inner mitochondrial membranes into the matrix. Unfolded proteins enter the mitochondrial matrix with a chaperone protein; the information required to target the precursor protein from the cytosol to the mitochondrial matrix is contained within its N-terminal matrix-targeting sequence. Translocation of precursors to the matrix occurs at the rare sites where the outer and inner membranes are close together." 
  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "ISBN:0716731363" 

- [protein import into mitochondrial matrix](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/GO_0030150) SubClassOf [protein transmembrane transport](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/GO_0071806) 

- [protein import into mitochondrial matrix](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/GO_0030150) SubClassOf [establishment of protein localization to organelle](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/GO_0072594) 

- [protein import into mitochondrial matrix](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/GO_0030150) SubClassOf [has target start location](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002338) some [cytosol](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/GO_0005829) 

- [protein import into mitochondrial matrix](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/GO_0030150) SubClassOf [has target end location](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/RO_0002339) some [mitochondrial matrix](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/GO_0005759)

- [secondary olfactory cortex](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0002895) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "Brodmann's area 28; major gateway for neocortical input to the hippocampus; origin of the perforant pathway; a component of the medial temporal lobe memory system. (CSP) * The cytoarchitecturally well-defined area of multilaminate cerebral cortex on the medial aspect of the parahippocampal gyrus, immediately caudal to the olfactory cortex of the uncus. The entorhinal cortex is the origin of the major neural fiber system afferent to the hippocampus, the so-called PERFORANT PATHWAY. (Stedman, 25th ed)." 
  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "BIRNLEX:2709"

### signal transduction `http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/GO_0007165`
#### Removed
- [signal transduction](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/GO_0007165) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "The cellular process in which a signal is conveyed to trigger a change in the activity or state of a cell. Signal transduction begins with reception of a signal (e.g. a ligand binding to a receptor or receptor activation by a stimulus such as light), or for signal transduction in the absence of ligand, signal-withdrawal or the activity of a constitutively active receptor. Signal transduction ends with regulation of a downstream cellular process, e.g. regulation of transcription or regulation of a metabolic process. Signal transduction covers signaling from receptors located on the surface of the cell and signaling via molecules located within the cell. For signaling between cells, signal transduction is restricted to events at and within the receiving cell." 
  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "GOC:go_curators" 

  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "GOC:mtg_signaling_feb11" 

#### Added
- [signal transduction](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/GO_0007165) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "The cellular process in which a signal is conveyed to trigger a change in the activity or state of a cell. Signal transduction begins with reception of a signal (e.g. a ligand binding to a receptor or receptor activation by a stimulus such as light), or for signal transduction in the absence of ligand, signal-withdrawal or the activity of a constitutively active receptor. Signal transduction ends with regulation of a downstream cellular process, e.g. regulation of transcription or regulation of a metabolic process. Signal transduction covers signaling from receptors located on the surface of the cell and signaling via molecules located within the cell. For signaling between cells, signal transduction is restricted to events at and within the receiving cell." 
  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "GOC:curators" 

  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "GOC:mtg_signaling_feb11"

### skeletal muscle organ, vertebrate `http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0014892`
#### Removed
- [skeletal muscle organ, vertebrate](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/UBERON_0014892) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "A muscle organ that consists of skeletal muscle tissue ensheathed in epimysium, that develops from myotome and that is innervated by some somatic motor neuron. Skeletal muscles are typically attached (via a tendon) to a bone but there are exceptions (e.g. intrinsic tongue muscles)."

### translation `http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/GO_0006412`
#### Removed
- [translation](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/GO_0006412) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "The cellular metabolic process in which a protein is formed, using the sequence of a mature mRNA or circRNA molecule to specify the sequence of amino acids in a polypeptide chain. Translation is mediated by the ribosome, and begins with the formation of a ternary complex between aminoacylated initiator methionine tRNA, GTP, and initiation factor 2, which subsequently associates with the small subunit of the ribosome and an mRNA or circRNA. Translation ends with the release of a polypeptide chain from the ribosome." 
  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "GOC:go_curators" 

#### Added
- [translation](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/GO_0006412) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "The cellular metabolic process in which a protein is formed, using the sequence of a mature mRNA or circRNA molecule to specify the sequence of amino acids in a polypeptide chain. Translation is mediated by the ribosome, and begins with the formation of a ternary complex between aminoacylated initiator methionine tRNA, GTP, and initiation factor 2, which subsequently associates with the small subunit of the ribosome and an mRNA or circRNA. Translation ends with the release of a polypeptide chain from the ribosome." 
  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "GOC:curators"

### ventral midline development `http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/GO_0007418`
#### Removed
- [ventral midline development](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/GO_0007418) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "The process whose specific outcome is the progression of the ventral midline over time, from its formation to the mature structure. In protostomes (such as insects, snails and worms) as well as deuterostomes (vertebrates), the midline is an embryonic region that functions in patterning of the adjacent nervous tissue. The ventral midline in insects is a cell population extending along the ventral surface of the embryo and is the region from which cells detach to form the ventrally located nerve cords. In vertebrates, the midline is originally located dorsally. During development, it folds inwards and becomes the ventral part of the dorsally located neural tube and is then called the ventral midline, or floor plate." 
  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "GOC:bf" 

  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "GOC:go_curators" 

  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "PMID:12075342" 

#### Added
- [ventral midline development](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/GO_0007418) [definition](http://purl.obolibrary.org/obo/IAO_0000115) "The process whose specific outcome is the progression of the ventral midline over time, from its formation to the mature structure. In protostomes (such as insects, snails and worms) as well as deuterostomes (vertebrates), the midline is an embryonic region that functions in patterning of the adjacent nervous tissue. The ventral midline in insects is a cell population extending along the ventral surface of the embryo and is the region from which cells detach to form the ventrally located nerve cords. In vertebrates, the midline is originally located dorsally. During development, it folds inwards and becomes the ventral part of the dorsally located neural tube and is then called the ventral midline, or floor plate." 
  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "GOC:curators" 

  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "GOC:bf" 

  - [database_cross_reference](http://www.geneontology.org/formats/oboInOwl#hasDbXref) "PMID:12075342"
